text: Hello, Catherine here from BBC Learning English.
zh: 大家好,我是BBC英语教学的凯瑟琳。

text: Just so you know, this programme is from the BBC Learning English Archive.
zh: 提醒大家,本节目来自BBC英语教学档案库。

text: It was originally broadcast in June 2008 on our website. Enjoy!
zh: 它最初于2008年6月在我们的网站上播出。祝大家听得开心!

text: bbclearningenglish .com
zh: bbclearningenglish.com

text: about something called ghostwriting.
zh: 关于一个叫做代笔写作的话题。

text: And with me to look at some of the language to do with this topical topic is Callum Robertson. Hi Callum.
zh: 和我一起探讨这个热门话题相关语言的是卡勒姆·罗伯逊。你好,卡勒姆。

text: Hi Amber. Callum, the verb to ghostwrite has nothing whatsoever to do with ghost stories, ghost
zh: 你好,安珀。卡勒姆,动词to ghostwrite跟鬼故事、鬼城,甚至幽灵列车都毫无关系,对吧?

text: towns or even ghost trains, does it? No, nothing at all.
zh: 没错,完全没有关系。

text: When we're talking about ghostwriting and to ghostwrite,
zh: 当我们说ghostwriting和to ghostwrite的时候,

text: it means to write a book or an article for another person.
zh: 它的意思是替别人写一本书或一篇文章。

text: So it seems that that person wrote that book, but actually somebody else wrote it for them. And these days it's quite common.
zh: 这样看起来好像是那个人自己写的书,但实际上是别人替他们写的。如今这种情况相当普遍。

text: I think many people might expect that books by celebrities particularly have actually been written by somebody else.
zh: 我想很多人可能都以为,尤其是名人写的书,其实是别人代笔写的。

text: They've been written by a ghostwriter. Now, today's question.
zh: 这些书是由代笔作家写的。好,现在来看今天的问题。

text: The word autobiography comes from the Greek for
zh: autobiography(自传)这个词源自希腊语中的

text: A, life pictures, B, life and
zh: A,生活图片;B,生活和

text: self, or C, self, life and right.
zh: 自我;还是C,自我、生活和书写。

text: Callum, what do you think? Well, I don't know ancient Greek or even modern Greek for that matter, but I would have a guess.
zh: 卡勒姆,你怎么看?我不懂古希腊语,甚至现代希腊语我也不懂,不过我可以猜一猜。

text: I think I can probably work this out because of auto, I think that's to do with the self.
zh: 我觉得我大概能推断出来,因为auto,我觉得这跟“自我”有关。

text: Bio is life and graphy, you know, it's graphology is writing.
zh: bio是“生活”,graphy呢,你知道,graphology是书写学的意思。

text: So I would say self, life and write. I think that's what autobiography means.
zh: 所以我认为是自我、生活和书写。我觉得这就是autobiography的意思。

text: Thank you. Well, we'll find out whether you're right at the end of the programme.
zh: 谢谢。好,我们会在节目最后揭晓你是否答对。

text: Now, the singer George Michael has recently signed a multi -million pound deal
zh: 歌手乔治·迈克尔最近签下了一份价值数百万英镑的合约,

text: for a no holds barred autobiography which he will write entirely himself
zh: 要写一本毫无保留的自传,而且完全由他本人亲自撰写,

text: in other words he's not going to use a ghost writer
zh: 换句话说,他不会使用代笔作家。

text: no holds barred Callum what does that three word adjective mean
zh: no holds barred,卡勒姆,这个三词形容词是什么意思?

text: well it's a good expression and here it means that nothing will be hidden
zh: 嗯,这是个很棒的表达,这里的意思是什么都不会被隐瞒。

text: if it's no holds barred everything will be revealed
zh: 如果说no holds barred,那就意味着一切都会被揭露出来。

text: now George Michael has had quite a colorful colourful life and no holds barred
zh: 乔治·迈克尔的人生相当精彩丰富,而这本毫无保留的

text: autobiography will be very interesting for many people to get his side of some of these colourful stories from his past.
zh: 自传对很多人来说会非常有意思,可以听到他自己讲述那些精彩往事的说法。

text: Thank you. Well whatever kind of book George Michael writes
zh: 谢谢。不过不管乔治·迈克尔写的是哪种书,

text: it's likely he will get some help from his agent and his publisher because that's how the publishing process works.
zh: 他很可能都会得到经纪人和出版商的一些帮助,因为出版行业的运作方式就是这样。

text: So why do celebrities use ghost writers to write their life stories.
zh: 那么,为什么名人要请代笔作家来写他们的人生故事呢?

text: Listen now to Joel Ricketts, an expert on the UK book industry.
zh: 现在我们来听听乔尔·里克茨的说法,他是英国图书行业的专家。

text: Why does he think a ghostwriter may make a better job of a celebrity autobiography than the celebrity themselves?
zh: 他为什么认为代笔作家写名人自传可能比名人自己写得更好?

text: Ghostwriters in many cases can actually capture the essence of the celebrity's character
zh: 在很多情况下,代笔作家其实能够更好地捕捉到名人性格的本质,

text: and their voice better than the celebrity could themselves if they were actually trying to write it.
zh: 以及他们的语气,比名人自己动笔写要写得更好。

text: What makes these books sell is the sense that you're sitting down
zh: 这些书之所以畅销,是因为让读者有一种坐下来的感觉——

text: Now, one -on -one, you're having a cup of tea or a beer with this celebrity, and you need to feel like it's really them that you know.
zh: 面对面地,好像在跟这位名人喝杯茶或喝杯啤酒一样,你需要感觉这真的是你认识的那个人。

text: And actually, if they're kind of trying to write it in their own stilted style, that doesn't work as well,
zh: 实际上,如果他们试图用自己那种生硬的风格来写,效果并不好,

text: whereas if you've got a professional there, they can actually capture that voice, that tone and that comedy,
zh: 而如果有专业人士在场,他们就能真正捕捉到那种语气、那种腔调和那种幽默感,

text: and, as I say, craft a book in a much more coherent way.
zh: 并且如我所说,以更连贯的方式打造出一本书。

text: So, Joel says there that ghostwriters often make a better job
zh: 所以乔尔在那里说,代笔作家写名人自传,

text: of a celebrity autobiography than the celebrity themselves.
zh: 往往比名人自己写得更好。

text: And he says that's because celebrities aren't professional writers
zh: 他说这是因为名人并不是专业作家,

text: and so they need help to be able to put their voice over on paper.
zh: 所以他们需要帮助才能把自己的声音呈现在纸面上。

text: And a professional writer can do that better than a professional celebrity.
zh: 而专业作家在这方面能做得比专业名人更好。

text: That's right. And the marketing of these best -selling celebrity autobiographies, or memoirs as they're sometimes called,
zh: 没错。而这些畅销的名人自传,有时也叫回忆录,在营销时,

text: is often about how intimate these books are, how they'll take you very close to the celebrity and reveal private and personal things about
zh: 往往强调这些书有多么私密,能让你多么贴近这位名人,揭示他们私人和个人的一面。

text: them. You're listening to bbclearningenglish
zh: 你正在收听bbclearningenglish

text: .com. Let's hear again from Joel, and this time he talks about readers' attitudes to ghosted celebrity memoirs.
zh: .com。让我们再听一次乔尔的话,这次他谈到读者对代笔名人回忆录的态度。

text: By the way, he says if you read the small print, you'll see the ghostwriter, or ghost, credited.
zh: 顺便说一下,他说如果你仔细看小字部分,你会看到代笔作家,也就是ghost,被署名致谢。

text: That means their name appears somewhere on the book to acknowledge their contribution.
zh: 这意味着他们的名字会出现在书里的某个地方,以此来承认他们的贡献。

text: As you listen, try to catch whether Joel thinks ghostwriting is becoming more acceptable to readers.
zh: 在听的时候,试着捕捉乔尔是否认为代笔写作正变得越来越被读者接受。

text: If they looked closely, they would see that the ghost is credited. I mean, it isn't...
zh: 如果他们仔细看,就会发现代笔作家是被署名致谢的。我是说,这并不是……

text: I think ghostwriting itself is becoming more mainstream.
zh: 我认为代笔写作本身正变得越来越主流。

text: The ghosts are sort of stepping out of the closet. In fact, Robert Harris has written a whole novel about a ghost.
zh: 代笔作家们正逐渐“走出衣柜”,不再遮遮掩掩。事实上,罗伯特·哈里斯就写过一整本关于代笔作家的小说。

text: And I think readers are getting a bit savvier. I think they don't mind.
zh: 而且我觉得读者也变得更精明了一些。我觉得他们并不介意,

text: as long as they understand that it's the celebrity's life, the celebrity's story, and it's still coming from the celebrity.
zh: 只要他们明白这是名人自己的人生、名人自己的故事,而且这些内容依然是出自这位名人本人。

text: So Joel says ghostwriting is becoming more mainstream. It's becoming less shocking because more people know about it.
zh: 所以乔尔说,代笔写作正变得越来越主流。它变得不再那么令人震惊,因为知道这件事的人越来越多。

text: And he says the ghosts are sort of stepping out of the closet. Can you explain that, Callum?
zh: 他说代笔作家们正“走出衣柜”。卡勒姆,你能解释一下这个说法吗?

text: Well, a closet is a wardrobe, somewhere you keep your clothes, and if something is in the closet, it means it's hidden.
zh: 嗯,closet是衣柜,是你放衣服的地方,如果某样东西“在衣柜里”,意思是它被藏起来了。

text: People don't know about it. But if something steps out of the closet, it becomes well known.
zh: 人们不知道它的存在。但如果某样东西“走出衣柜”,那就意味着它变得广为人知了。

text: It becomes not hidden anymore. So the ghosts are stepping out of the closet.
zh: 它不再被隐藏了。所以代笔作家们正在“走出衣柜”。

text: So people are now aware. They know that there are ghost writers and they accept that there are ghost writers.
zh: 所以现在人们意识到了这一点。他们知道有代笔作家的存在,也接受代笔作家的存在。

text: Now it's time to say, Callum, you got the right answer to our question at the beginning of the programme.
zh: 现在是时候公布了,卡勒姆,你在节目开头的那个问题上答对了。

text: The word autobiography comes from the Greek for self, life and right.
zh: autobiography这个词源自希腊语,意思是自我、生活和书写。

text: Do join us next time for more 6 Minute English. Goodbye.
zh: 欢迎下次继续收听《六分钟英语》。再见。
